title: BUG: retrieve()'s dense L3 pass ignores active_until - expired facts resurrect via embeddings; also uses raw importance, bypassing decay
source: forgetting audit 2026-07-13 (hackathon Track 1 criterion: timely forgetting)
current_state: The dense-cosine L3 query inside MemoryTools.retrieve() (tools_memory.py ~line 832) selects 'WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL AND invalid_at IS NULL AND (superseded_by IS NULL OR superseded_by = '')' - NO active_until filter. Every other L3 read path (_search_l3_cache, _l3_fast_path, _hybrid_search_l3) filters '(active_until IS NULL OR active_until > now)'. So a fact that expired (or was decay-archived, which sets active_until=now) but carries an embedding is RESURRECTED by dense retrieval - forgetting is silently undone by the newest read path. Classic write/read divergence (the project's #1 recurring bug class). Additionally retrieve()'s importance term uses raw importance/10 instead of memory.salience.effective_importance(), so Ebbinghaus decay never influences fused ranking.
target_state: (1) The dense L3 query carries the same active_until filter as every other L3 read: AND (active_until IS NULL OR active_until > ?) with now_iso. (2) retrieve()'s importance term uses effective_importance(importance, decay_rate, last_accessed_at, pinned)/10 - decayed rows rank lower, pinned rows immune - which requires selecting decay_rate/last_accessed_at/pinned in both the lexical-candidate dict build and the dense query (wrap in try/except sqlite3.OperationalError falling back to raw importance, matching the existing pre-migration-schema pattern).
migration_plan: 1. Add now_iso + active_until predicate to the dense L3 SELECT in retrieve().
2. Extend that SELECT with decay_rate, last_accessed_at, pinned; compute importance_norm via memory.salience.effective_importance (raw-importance fallback on OperationalError).

3. Reproducing test (the proof): write an L3 fact with expiry in the past via memory_write(expiry=...), give it an embedding (patch compute_embedding_for_write + backfill), then retrieve() with a paraphrase query mocked to embed identically - assert the expired row is NOT returned. Without the fix this test fails.
4. Second test: a decayed row (old last_accessed_at, high decay_rate) ranks below a fresh row of equal importance when PI-decay fields present.
5. Existing test_hybrid_retriever.py + test_memory_roundtrip_contract.py stay green; python scripts/verify.py PASS.
risk_notes: Keep the fix inside retrieve() only - do NOT refactor the other read paths into a shared query builder in this ticket (tempting, but the gate must stay green during hackathon week; file a follow-up if desired). The decay-aware importance term changes ranking only when decay fields exist and differ; equal-field rows rank identically to today.
